summary: Excellent soundboard recording of an energetic 1987 Shoreline show, highlighted by a smoking Hey Pocky Way, Candyman and the seamless Maggie’s Farm→Cumberland Blues transition.
review: The concert is praised for its high‑energy first set – Hey Pocky Way, Minglewood Blues and a blistering Candyman – and an especially tight second‑set opener of Maggie’s Farm into Cumberland Blues. Reviewers note the band was “on” all night with Jerry’s fire‑filled guitar work, Brent’s playful keys, and a deep groove from Phil, while the soundboard source (remastered by Jay Ashley) sounds superb and crystal‑clear.

ai_rating.rationale: The overwhelming majority of reviews are 4–5 stars, emphasizing strong musical performance and excellent soundboard quality; only a few minor criticisms keep the rating just below top‑tier.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remastered by Jay Ashley; reviewers describe the mix as superb, clear and well‑balanced with great acoustics from Shoreline. No significant drop‑outs or muddiness reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Short but tight two‑set show; first set launches with a breezy Hey Pocky Way and builds to an emotional Bird Song, while the second set kicks off with a seamless Maggie’s Farm→Cumberland Blues transition that keeps the momentum high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as ‘on fire’, with crisp tone, soaring solos on Candyman and Hey Pocky Way; many note his guitar effects made the performance especially spacey.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bottom‑end praised for deep groove and solid bass foundation that listeners could “listen to Phil” on.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s (Weir) playing highlighted on Minglewood Blues and Masterpiece, with strong interplay with Jerry.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland lauded for playful, distinctive keyboards – especially at the end of Maggie’s Farm and throughout the second set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming noted as creative during drums/space sections; no major criticism.
